prose:
Computed condensed Fukui reactivity indices for phenol.

Method provenance:
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor; most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites identified.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) via PySCF 2.x backend.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied phenol.xyz coordinates (no re-optimization at DFT; protocol is DFT//input-geometry).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Electronic state: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1) for the N-electron reference; cation and anion states computed at charge +1/–1, multiplicity 2 for the N±1 steps.
Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. Integrity checks report "sum(f⁺) = 1.0000" and "sum(f⁻) = 1.0000" (within expected tolerance); overall status "ok" and trustworthy=true. No engine warnings were issued.

Headline result:
The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (C, index 2) with condensed Fukui f⁺ = 2.1028319 (unitless). This value is provided under "value" above.
The most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.16201477. Full f⁺/f⁻/dual-descriptor arrays and a visualization PNG are available in the output JSON and plot file at:
/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/20260625-131609_phenol_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
Live .out log (recorded during execution) was streamed to the working directory; re-run command captured in the result JSON (see cli_invocation).

No experimental comparison is provided since the user requested only the computed indices.
